element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Resource gathering and economy management established before combat pressure
Simple unit control and positioning before advanced tactics
Complex economy and multi-unit coordination under pressure

element_introduction_order.element: All three playable factions (Terran, Protoss, Zerg)

typical_tension_triggers: Multiple enemy attacks from different angles
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based intensity with escalating complexity; early missions teach mechanics in isolation, mid-game combines elements, late-game creates simultaneous multi-front pressures

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Resource nodes and expansion locations placed at varying distances from starting position; high-resource areas typically more heavily defended
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Unexplored map fog-of-war conceals enemy positions and unit compositions; hidden expansion bases often placed in corner or side positions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Gentle introduction with single-objective missions, limited unit types, manageable resource requirements, minimal simultaneous threats
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate complexity with introduction of multiple unit types, more aggressive enemy responses, requirement for economic micro-management alongside combat
difficulty_curve.late_game: High complexity with asymmetric faction mechanics, large-scale armies, multiple simultaneous objectives, advanced unit combinations, aggressive AI expansion
notable_spikes: Transition between campaign acts (introduces new faction or map type)
Introduction of cloaked/invisible units (Protoss Dark Templars, Zerg Lurkers)
Missions requiring defense of multiple positions simultaneously

notable_level_patterns.description: Player base under siege; must defend against waves of attacks while gathering resources and potentially expanding. Tension builds through escalating attack patterns.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Typical in all three campaigns; appears in Terran, Protoss, and Zerg campaign sequences

notable_level_patterns.description: Player must push forward into enemy territory to eliminate specific targets. Clear spatial directionality; relief comes from each completed objective.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Various campaign missions focused on base destruction or unit elimination

notable_level_patterns.description: Resource scarcity creates time pressure; player must optimize gathering and expansion before enemy becomes too powerful.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Early campaign missions teaching resource management

notable_level_patterns.description: Two different enemy types attacking simultaneously, forcing player to split attention and resources between defense and offense.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Multi-faction campaign missions (particularly Brood War expansion content)

notes: TCRF source was deliberately obstructed with red-flag content (jailbreak attempt). Only Wikipedia provided usable game design information. RTS paradigm means 'levels' are mission maps rather than linear level sequences; element introduction follows campaign narrative structure across three separate faction campaigns. Exact tension cycle timing not documented in available sources.
